$_comment: BAD EXAMPLE: Slides are too dense, headlines are topics, citations are missing, template choices are wrong.
title: Business Update

slides.headline: Financial Overview Including Revenue Margins Costs Headcount and Regional Breakdowns

items: Total revenue for the quarter came in at twenty nine point three million dollars which represents an eighteen percent increase compared to the same quarter last year and exceeded the plan by four percentage points

Gross margins expanded by two hundred and ten basis points reaching sixty four point four percent driven primarily by infrastructure optimization projects completed in North America and the EMEA region
Operating expenses increased by twelve percent due to headcount additions in engineering and the APAC sales team expansion that was approved in the previous quarter
APAC region pipeline grew thirty one percent quarter over quarter making it the fastest growing segment for the second consecutive quarter
Customer acquisition cost in EMEA increased twenty three percent over two months with root cause still under investigation by the go to market team
The data pipeline currently relies on a single supplier handling seventy one percent of volume with contract renewal in August requiring immediate attention
We are requesting board approval for eighteen new roles across engineering data science and APAC sales at a total annual cost of two point four million dollars

$_failures: slide s2: headline is 13 words — exceeds 10-word max and reads like a table of contents, not a takeaway
slide s2: 7 bullets with 30+ words each — body text far exceeds 70-word cap (actual: ~180 words)
slide s2: no source_citations on a content slide — schema validation should reject this
slide s2: all information crammed into one slide instead of 4-5 slides with focused messaging
slide s2: no template variety — KPIs should use kpi.big, risks should use headline.evidence, hiring should use headline.evidence
slide s3: headline='Summary' is a topic label, not a takeaway. Should state the closing action.
slide s3: summary bullets are vague platitudes ('Good quarter', 'Some risks') — not actionable
All slides: speaker_notes are empty — violates the rubric for content and summary slides
Title slide: subtitle is just 'Update' — provides no context about the meeting or date
Only 3 slides total for an 8-slide brief — massive underdelivery on content
